title: Fixes #12442: Add 3D Generation to the Model Monitor
author: sharktide

gist.headline: The monitor grew depth
gist.blurb: The Model Monitor has learned a new dimension: 3D generation. Polly brought tiny wizard glasses, because apparently flat dashboards were not enough.
gist.summary: Added 3D generation to the community-built Model Monitor, so its model listings and monitoring hooks now include 3D-capable models.
gist.impact: Model Monitor users can now discover and track 3D generation alongside image, text, audio, and video models from the same cozy control room.
gist.visual_concept: Depict the Model Monitor as a friendly retro CRT dashboard gaining a small rotating pixel-art 3D cube or sculpted object. Polly, wearing tiny wizard glasses, adjusts the new 3D control while code-plants and flowers grow around the monitor; the monitor robot can enthusiastically inspect the dimensional model.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a warm garden hackerspace where Polly, the cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings, wears tiny wizard glasses while tuning a retro Model Monitor CRT that displays a rotating 3D pixel cube; a helpful monitor robot points at it as code-plants bloom from lime green (#ecf874) pots. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, with subtle pixel-font text: "3D ONLINE"; nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
